hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: Your cards — 5, 9. Both players called a raise before the cards were dealt.
Turn — Ace. Your opponent led out with a bet. You called.
River — Jack. Your opponent fired again — half the pot.

wrongReasons: 2 3 makes a flush here — a hand that strong would have raised earlier and pushed harder for maximum value. The measured sizing in this hand points to something weaker.
6 6 has little reason to lead the river here — one pair can't comfortably go for value after calling two streets.
4 J has little reason to lead the river here — two pair can't comfortably go for value after calling two streets.
hand.explanation:
Answer: 10 8

Your opponent flopped a straight and immediately raised to build the pot. They led every remaining street — consistent value betting across all streets is a strong signal of a made hand.
